original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49.

evidence.reasoning: Quote literally names F1 as the blown input fuse; FUNCTIONAL CANDIDATE MAP maps F1 → F1 in graph.
Blown SMD fuse = open circuit mode.

evidence.source_quote_substring: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity
evidence.reasoning: F1 is source_refdes for VIN per graph; an open F1 kills VIN, consistent with no rail activity.

evidence.source_quote_substring: no voltage reaching sense resistor R49
evidence.reasoning: R49 sits downstream of F1 on the input path; with F1 open, no voltage reaches R49.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Blown SMD input fuse F1 opens the VIN path, causing total board-dead symptom; rail VIN sourced by F1 in graph cascades.

detail: cause.refdes='C130' does not appear in the fetched content of 'https://mntre.com/reform2/handbook/advanced.html' — likely cross-source contamination (refdes literal in dump but not in cited URL)

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: SD card boot fails; system won't read SD card even though LPC and 3.3 V main rail are confirmed live. Likely cause: 1.8 V rail dead — level shifters U28 and U8 require both 3.3 V and 1.8 V to operate, blocking the SD interface. Components mentioned: U28, U8 (level shifters). Rail / test point: 1.8 V rail at C130.

evidence.reasoning: C130 is the 1.8V rail decoupling cap test point; map C130→C130.
Leaky cap loading the rail until it collapses.
Typical leaky-MLCC short resistance ~200 Ω.
Level shifters fail without 1.8V; U28 in graph (U8 not present).

original_draft.reasoning_summary: Leaky C130 collapses the 1.8V rail; SD level shifters U28 lose 1.8V side. (1.8V rail not in main rail list with C130 source so omitted.)

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. External HDMI may or may not work. Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate. Rail / test point: 1.2 V at C37.

evidence.reasoning: C37 is the 1.2V test cap; map C37→C37.
Leaky cap pulling rail down.
Typical leaky-MLCC short resistance ~150 Ω.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Internal display dark or no video
evidence.reasoning: +1V2 rail in graph has decoupling=C37; loading C37 collapses +1V2; symptoms (display dark) consistent.

evidence.source_quote_substring: TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10)
evidence.reasoning: U9 and U10 both require the 1.2V rail.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: C37 leaky-short collapses +1V2 rail; both U9 (USB hub) and U10 (DSI bridge) lose their core supply, killing internal display and USB.

original_draft.source_quote: Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate. Probe C37 for 1.2 V first; if absent, diode-mode on nearby decoupling caps to detect short.

original_draft.source_quote: Symptom: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly; HDMI output unaffected. Likely cause: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) between CPU module and SN65DSI86 eDP bridge, or Wi-Fi antenna routing interference into display path. reflow U10 if suspected cold joint.

evidence.reasoning: U10 named literally as the cold-joint suspect; map U10→U10.
Cold joint → effectively dead component (signal-side failure, no rail collapse).

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U10 cold joint is a local signal-integrity failure with no rail cascade; HDMI unaffected per quote — zero-cascade scenario.

detail: cause.refdes='U14' does not appear in the fetched content of 'https://community.mnt.re/t/debugging-dead-motherboard/970' — likely cross-source contamination (refdes literal in dump but not in cited URL)

original_draft.source_quote: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC.

evidence.reasoning: U14 named literally; map U14→U14.
Partial-failure mode: regulator falling out of regulation, undervoltage on LPC_VCC.
Brown-out at ~80% of nominal (≈2.6 V) is a typical regulator-degraded signature.
U14 is the LPC_VCC source per graph; under-regulation drags rail below brown-out threshold.
U18 brown-outs at undervoltage.

original_draft.reasoning_summary: U14 regulating low (≈2.6 V) browns out the LPC_VCC rail; U18 fails to run, D11 dim/off.
